evals.prompt: Generate research questions for this ticket: 'Add CSV export to the orders dashboard. Agents need to download filtered order lists for month-end reconciliation. Must respect the existing column visibility settings.'
evals.expected_output: A questions artifact with 5-10 targeted, current-state questions tagged by category, a human-only Context section, a determined compatibility posture, rendered via Skill('output-html') to research/questions/YYYY-MM-DD-topic.html
expectations: Produces 5-10 questions total, each beginning with a category tag (e.g. [data-model], [call-graph], [entry-points])
Every question is about the codebase as it exists today (how exports/downloads currently work, where column visibility settings live), never 'how should we build X'
Does NOT read any codebase files, grep, or glob — the only input read is the ticket itself
Section 01 Context contains the human-only callout stating the section is stripped before being passed to /research-codebase

Determines the compatibility posture (breaking_changes_allowed) by inference or a single AskUserQuestion, and records crispy:breaking_changes_allowed + crispy:compatibility_context in META_EXTRA with a matching Breaking changes SUMMARY_EXTRA row
Delegates rendering to Skill('output-html') with PHASE=questions and STATUS=ready-for-research, writing to research/questions/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>
Presents the questions for review, offers to commit via gh-commit, and does NOT proceed to research automatically

evals.prompt: Generate research questions for this request using --format=md: 'Our background jobs sometimes process the same message twice. Investigate what we'd need to know before adding idempotency.'
evals.expected_output: A Markdown questions artifact whose questions force exploration of the current job/queue infrastructure, with no implementation opinions, written to research/questions/YYYY-MM-DD-topic.md
expectations: Because --format=md was passed, delegates to Skill('output-markdown') and writes research/questions/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>.md
Questions target current state: how jobs are dispatched and consumed, where message handling lives, existing dedup/retry behavior, test patterns for job handlers
No question asks for a design opinion ('what's the best way', 'how should') and none names specific files to change
Questions are pruned to 5-10 — vague or redundant candidates are cut rather than padded
Does not interrogate the user for more feature context; the only permitted user question is the compatibility posture when it cannot be inferred
Output uses the shared slot vocabulary (PHASE, STATUS, TOPIC, TITLE, TICKET, PROMPT_TEXT, META_EXTRA, SUMMARY_EXTRA) without inventing new slots
